skill.name: C4 Container Level: System Deployment
skill.description: Expert C4 Container-level documentation specialist. Synthesizes Component-level documentation into Container-level architecture, mapping components to deployment units, documenting container interfaces as APIs, and creating container diagrams.
skill.summary: Creates C4 container-level architecture documentation with deployment diagrams, API specifications, and container interface mappings.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a prompt-only documentation assistant with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found 0 security issues. The skill provides templates and guidance for C4 container-level architecture documentation, including Mermaid diagram syntax and OpenAPI specification templates. No network access, file system operations, or external command execution present.

content.value_statement: Transform component-level designs into deployment-ready container architecture. Generate container diagrams, API specifications, and deployment documentation using the C4 model standard.
actual_capabilities: Maps software components to deployment containers with clear boundaries
Creates C4 container diagrams using Mermaid syntax
Documents container interfaces as OpenAPI/Swagger specifications
Defines container dependencies and external system integrations
Provides deployment configuration templates for Docker and Kubernetes
Documents infrastructure requirements including scaling and resources
limitations: Requires existing component-level documentation as input
Does not generate actual deployment infrastructure or code
Focused on C4 model methodology only
Does not validate actual deployment configurations against live systems

use_cases.title: Microservices Architecture Documentation
use_cases.description: Document container boundaries and API contracts for a microservices-based system deployment.

use_cases.title: Legacy System Modernization
use_cases.description: Map existing monolithic components to containerized deployment units for cloud migration.

use_cases.title: System Integration Planning
use_cases.description: Document container interfaces and external system dependencies for integration teams.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Container Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a C4 container diagram for [system name] that includes [list key containers]. Show the Person actors, container types, and communication protocols between them.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new system design with basic container requirements

prompt_templates.title: Component to Container Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: I have these components: [list components]. Map them to deployment containers following C4 model principles. Include container types, technologies, and deployment strategies for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting logical component architecture to physical deployment units

prompt_templates.title: API Specification Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an OpenAPI 3.1 specification for the [container name] container. It exposes these endpoints: [list endpoints with descriptions]. Include request/response schemas.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating formal API documentation for container interfaces

prompt_templates.title: Full Container Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create complete C4 container documentation for [system]. Include: container diagram in Mermaid syntax, all container definitions with technologies, API specifications, dependencies, external systems, and infrastructure requirements for [deployment target].
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive container-level architecture documentation for production deployment

output_examples.input: Create a container diagram for an e-commerce platform with web frontend, API backend, database, and payment service.
output: C4Container diagram showing Person(customer) -> Web Application -> API Application -> Database and Payment Service
Container definitions with technology stacks: React frontend, Node.js API, PostgreSQL database, Stripe payment integration
Interface documentation: REST endpoints between web and API, SQL queries to database, HTTPS calls to payment gateway
Deployment notes: Docker containers orchestrated via Kubernetes with horizontal scaling for API tier

output_examples.input: Document the API specification for a user authentication container.
output: Request/response schemas for JWT token handling
Security scheme definitions for Bearer token authentication
Server configuration for production and staging environments

best_practices: Always define clear container boundaries based on deployment autonomy and scaling requirements
Document communication protocols explicitly between all containers and external systems
Include both technical details and deployment characteristics for each container
anti_patterns: Creating containers that mirror component structure without considering deployment needs
Omitting external system dependencies and third-party integrations from diagrams
Documenting container APIs without formal specifications or endpoint definitions

faq.question: What is the C4 Container level and when should I use it?
faq.answer: The C4 Container level shows how a system is made up of multiple interacting containers like applications, data stores, and microservices. Use it when you need to document deployment architecture and technology choices for your system.

faq.question: How does Container level differ from Component level?
faq.answer: Container level focuses on deployment units and runtime containers with technology choices. Component level breaks down each container into logical code components. Container is about where code runs; Component is about how code is organized.

faq.question: What container types should I include in my diagram?
faq.answer: Include Web Applications, API Applications, Mobile Apps, Databases (ContainerDb), Message Queues (Container_Queue), File Systems, and external systems. Show all containers that are part of your deployment architecture.

faq.question: How detailed should the API specifications be?
faq.answer: Include all public endpoints with HTTP methods, request parameters, response schemas, and authentication requirements. Use OpenAPI 3.1 format for standardization and tool compatibility.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for existing systems?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide existing deployment documentation, Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, or architecture descriptions. The skill will synthesize this into C4 container documentation format.

faq.question: What communication protocols should I document?
faq.answer: Document protocols like HTTPS, gRPC, SQL, messaging protocols (AMQP, MQTT), or any inter-container communication. Include the protocol name and data format (JSON, Protobuf, etc.) where applicable.
